evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: knowing when to ship a quick fix in a day and when to architect a quarter-long project
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: velocity signals ('ship a quick fix in a day', 'speed up data investigation', 'work faster') sit alongside rigour signals ('analytical correctness as non-negotiable', code reviews, CI) at roughly equal weight.
evidence.domain_risk: As the most trusted platform for measuring online behavior, our data is only as powerful as our ability to communicate it.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Public company (NYSE-listed) selling digital intelligence data to enterprise customers, but the JD's own language is about analytical correctness and trust rather than financial reporting or regulatory compliance, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: As the sole engineering role on the team, you will own the technical work end-to-end
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A public, 4,300-customer company with an established Data Enablement team and named platform stack (Databricks, GitLab) reflects a mature organization, even though this specific engineering seat is newly created.
evidence.jd_authorship: Own the Databricks development environment and project structure for the team.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Databricks, GitLab, PySpark, AWS) and precise day-to-day scenarios make the actual work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: help build the crucial bridge between our data and our customer-facing organization
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role primarily serves analysts and the data function's own tooling/infrastructure needs, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Can take a vague, messy problem and drive it to a working solution by planning, designing, and delivering independently.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: As the sole engineer on the team, the role owns end-to-end technical direction and judgement calls independently — clearly strategic.
evidence.ai_role: Use AI coding assistants (like Cursor or Claude) as a normal, everyday part of your workflow.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude) as a routine part of their own work.
evidence.testing_framing: Has a solid grasp of software engineering fundamentals (Git, continuous integration, and the discipline to maintain a mostly one-person codebase).
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: CI and engineering discipline appear as a requirement/skill rather than an explicit owned testing or data-quality practice with ownership verbs.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: None: the JD is framed around delivery, tool-building, and analytical correctness as craft quality rather than compliance or stakeholder-trust risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Own the Databricks development environment and project structure for the team.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new tools/infrastructure (greenfield) and productionizing/automating existing analyst work (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid work model: 3 days in our office and 2 days working from home
